Seasonal · Live fire · Hayes Valley

A quiet
kind of fire

Sorrel is a forty-two-seat restaurant cooking the day's market over oak and almond wood — paired with a short, considered list of natural wine.

Reserve a table Read the menu
4.8★Live on Google
Tue–SunFrom 5pm
100miSourcing radius
2019Est.

Our story

A market stall and a borrowed grill, grown into a neighborhood table.

We cook almost everything over live fire — the flavor a wood fire gives can't be faked. Bread is milled and baked in-house, vegetables arrive twice a week from growers we know, and we use the whole animal so nothing is wasted. No dress code, no theatre.

Daily

The menu is rewritten each morning

In-house

Milled, baked & butchered on site

Low-waste

Whole-animal, root-to-leaf cooking

The card, this week

Written by the market

01
Pasta

Hand-cut tagliatelle

A slow ragù of the day, brown butter, and whatever herbs came in the box this morning.

02
From the fire

Whole fish on the coals

Line-caught and grilled over oak until the skin crackles. Salsa verde, lemon, sea salt.

03
Garden

Charred little gems

Anchovy, aged sheep's cheese, toasted breadcrumb. Simple, green, a little smoky.

Book the hearth menu

Why Sorrel

Four quiet convictions

Live fire

Oak & almond wood, no gas line.

Market-led

The menu changes with the harvest.

Natural wine

A short, low-intervention list.

Neighborhood

Walk-ins welcome at the bar.

How a night works

Three small steps

1

Reserve

Book a table or counter seat online. Bar kept open for walk-ins.

2

Arrive

Settle in with a glass. Tell us about any allergies.

3

Be fed

Order à la carte or hand the night to the kitchen.

The room

Inside Sorrel

The hearth Tonight's pasta The room Natural pours Morning box Detail The bar Levain

Good to know

Questions

01Do you take walk-ins?+
Yes — the bar and a handful of counter seats are kept for walk-ins every night. The dining room books up, so reserve ahead for a table at a set time.
02Allergies & dietary needs?+
Always. Tell us when you book or sit down. Because we cook to the market we can flex most dishes, with strong vegetable and gluten-free options nightly.
03Is there a tasting menu?+
Yes — the Hearth Menu is a chef's selection of the day's best, served family-style. Book the counter to watch it come together.
04Do you do corkage?+
We do, for a per-bottle fee, on bottles not on our list. Share a taste with the team and it's on us.
05Where should I park?+
Street parking around Hayes Valley plus the Civic Center garage two blocks east. Short walk from Van Ness Muni; a bike rack out front.

Private dining & events

We'll cook across the city

Buy out the room or bring the hearth to you — dinners and events around the Bay:

Hayes ValleyNoPaThe MissionPacific HeightsMission BayOakland
